dslRule:
<!id: use-diamond-operator>
<!description: Replace explicit type arguments with the diamond operator where type can be inferred by the compiler (Java 7+).
This transformation is only applied if the compiler infers the same type with the diamond operator, ensuring semantic equivalence.>
<!severity: HINT>

new $Type<$Exprs$>()
=>
new $Type<>()
;;
summary: Replaces explicit type arguments in constructor calls (e.g., `new ArrayList<String>()`) with the diamond operator (`new ArrayList<>()`) if type inference allows for identical type derivation. This is a common Java 7+ modernization.

verification.message: Candidate DSL must contain only one rule and optional per-rule metadata; file metadata, includes, and embedded Java are not promotable
